steps.title: Write the side relationship
steps.content:
In an isosceles right triangle, if each leg is $a$, the hypotenuse is $a\sqrt{2}$. The perimeter is
$$
a+a+a\sqrt{2}=a(2+\sqrt{2})
$$

steps.title: Solve for the leg
steps.content: Set the perimeter expression equal to the given perimeter. $$ a(2+\sqrt{2})=86+86\sqrt{2} $$ Since $$ 43\sqrt{2}(2+\sqrt{2})=86\sqrt{2}+86 $$ the leg length matches the given perimeter. The answer is B.
